_comment: District of Columbia Retirement Board (DCRB) pension data for DC Police Officers and Firefighters Retirement Plan and DC Teachers Retirement Plan. Consumer-agnostic. No Meridian-specific references. Most DC government employees hired after 1987 are covered by FERS. DCRB covers MPD officers, FEMS firefighters, and DCPS ET-classified teachers only.
administeredBy: DC Retirement Board (DCRB) — independent DC agency. Federal benefit payments for pre-July 1, 1997 service managed by US Treasury Office of DC Pensions (ODCP). Benefits jointly administered by DCRB and Treasury.
scopingNote: DCRB covers only MPD police officers, FEMS firefighters, and DCPS teachers (ET-classified). Most DC government employees are in FERS (federal). DC workers hired on or after October 1, 1987 are in FERS; pre-October 1, 1987 may be in CSRS. FERS/CSRS data is in the federal data files in this repo.

federalBenefitSplit.description: Under the National Capital Revitalization and Self-Government Improvement Act of 1997 (Balanced Budget Act, Title XI), the federal government assumed DC unfunded pension liability. US Treasury funds benefits for service before July 1, 1997; DC government funds benefits for service on or after July 1, 1997. DCRB administers both portions.
federalBenefitSplit.authority: Balanced Budget Act of 1997, Public Law 105-33, Title XI

policeFire.planName: District of Columbia Police Officers and Firefighters Retirement Plan
covers: DC Metropolitan Police Department (MPD) sworn officers
DC Fire and Emergency Medical Services (FEMS) uniformed firefighters and EMTs

socialSecurity.note: Police officers and firefighters are not covered by Social Security. This plan is their primary retirement benefit.

tiers._note: Three tiers by hire date. One-time irrevocable voluntary tier change allowed at full actuarial cost (Retirement Options Act, D.C. Law 17-224).

formula.structure: 2.5% x Average Base Pay x Service up to 20 years + 3.0% x Average Base Pay x Service beyond 20 years

averageBasePay.description: Highest annual pay averaging over any 12 consecutive months

cola.note: Uncapped CPI-based COLA for Tier 1. Pre-February 15, 1980 retirees receive equalization pay rather than COLA.

formula.structure: 2.5% x Average Base Pay x Service up to 25 years + 3.0% x Average Base Pay x Service beyond 25 years

averageBasePay.description: Highest annual pay averaging over any 36 consecutive months. Includes longevity pay for officers with 25+ years (police) or firefighters.

normalRetirement.note: 25 years of service and at least age 50

mandatoryRetirement.note: Mandatory at age 60; Mayor may grant extensions

formula.structure: 2.5% x Average Base Pay x Total Creditable Service

averageBasePay.description: Highest annual pay averaging over any 36 consecutive months

exampleBenefit.scenario: Officer with 25 years service, $100,000 average base pay

normalRetirement.note: 25 years at any age — no minimum age requirement

mandatoryRetirement.note: Mandatory at age 60; Mayor may grant extensions

tier3.unusedSickLeave: Added to service for benefit calculation; not used for retirement eligibility or longevity pay

policeFire.survivorBenefit: Standard survivor benefit provided automatically. Optional enhanced survivor benefit at retirement reduces member benefit by 10%; survivor receives increased benefit.
policeFire.lineOfDutyDisability: Two-thirds of average pay if higher than formula-based benefit

teachers.planName: District of Columbia Teachers Retirement Plan
covers: DCPS teachers and instructional employees in ET-classified positions (salary grades ET 1-15)
Former DCPS teachers who transferred within 60 days to a DC public charter school and elected to continue
excludes: Non-ET classified DCPS employees (covered by FERS or CSRS)
Charter school teachers not previously DCPS employees

socialSecurity.note: DCPS teachers hired on or after October 1, 1987 participate in Social Security. Teachers hired before October 1, 1987 may be under CSRS (non-SS). This plan is in addition to Social Security for most current teachers.

tiers._note: Two tiers based on hire date relative to November 1, 1996. Difference: formula multiplier structure, contribution rate, COLA cap.

formula.structure: Tiered multiplier: 1.5% x Average Salary x first 5 years + 1.75% x Average Salary x years 5-10 + 2.0% x Average Salary x years beyond 10

averageSalary.description: Highest annual pay averaging over any 36 consecutive months of teaching service

formula.structure: 2.0% x Average Salary x Years of Service

averageSalary.description: Highest annual pay averaging over any 36 consecutive months of teaching service

normalRetirement.rule: Any age with 30 years of service

retirementEligibility.deferredRetirement: Terminated vested members (5+ years) may receive deferred annuity at age 62, or lump-sum refund of contributions

colaHistory._note: Both plans base COLA on prior-year CPI change. Effective March 1; payable April 1. Post-1996/Tier 3 cap is 3%; pre-1996/Tier 1 may be uncapped.

sources.name: DCRB — Police and Fire Summary Plan FAQs
DCRB — Police/Fire Statement of Estimated Benefits FAQs
DCRB — Teachers Statement of Estimated Benefits FAQs

researchGaps: Exact active member counts by plan (not prominently published)
Total fund assets — available in ODCP Annual Reports at treasury.gov
DC Judges Retirement Plan — administered by Treasury ODCP, not DCRB; would need separate file
Pre-1996 teacher tiered multiplier exact breakpoints — full details in Teachers Plan SPD 2023 at treasury.gov
